markdown.markdown:
:::{#hero-heading} **Cognitive Psychologist | Experimentalist | Technologist** I am a Cognitive Experimental Psychologist from Te Whanganui-a-Tara, Aotearoa | Wellington, New Zealand. I conduct research at the intersection of cognition and emotion. The experiments I design investigate such questions as: "Is our cognitive control (e.g., inhibition, attentional control, planning) enhanced or hindered when we experience fear?" and "Can a mind construct human-like concepts of emotions if that mind has no physical body?" Training & Background: My professional training is a story spanning 13 years and several disciplines of psychology. In 2013, I graduated from Loyola University of Maryland with a BA in Psychology. I was then employed at the Pediatric Feeding Disorders Unit at The Kennedy Krieger Institute. In my role as a behavior data specialist, I was trained to implement and refine treatments based on principles of Applied Behaviour Analysis. In 2015, I moved to Aotearoa to pursue a PhD in Cognitive Developmental Psychology at Te Herenga Waka | Victoria University of Wellington. In my doctoral research, I developed an ecologically valid behavioural test capable of disentangling competing theoretical accounts about the cognitive mechanisms that allow humans to track the content of other people's minds. Following graduation, my research interests evolved again. I started to focus on how experimental psychology has confronted *ecological validity* (i.e., the extent to which laboratory findings can inform us about phenomena that occurs outside the laboratory). I became increasingly skeptical of what was historically considered an inescapable trade-off: that it is not possible to have complete control over experimental conditions without stripping those conditiosn of their verisimilitude (i.e., the extent to which laboratory conditions replicate conditions of the real-world). My central thesis was that that this apparent trade-off was not a *logical* incompatibility, but simply a *practical* challenge, wanting for a technological solution. I further believed that the technological solution had just come to market, in the form of the virtual reality headset. From 2020 to 2023, I speerheaded the development of a virtual reality laboratory for the advancement of a more *ecologically valid experimental psychology*. I **human factors and ergonomics**, **ecologically valid experimentation**, **VR and simulation science**, and the emerging challenge of **human-AI hybrid workflow design**. I use tools like **virtual reality**, **mobile biosensors**, and **data analytics** to deliver evidence that holds up under scrutiny—not opinions. Whether you need a usability evaluation, a controlled experiment, a VR-based training prototype, or a strategy for integrating agentic AI into your team's workflow without losing what makes human judgement irreplaceable—I provide end-to-end [consulting services](consulting.qmd): from problem framing and study design to data collection, analysis, and clear recommendations your team can implement. [Hire Me for Consulting](contact.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [View Publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [ Download Academic CV](cv/Christopher_Maymon_Academic_CV_2026.docx){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} --- :::
## How I Can Help ::: {.grid} ::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6} ### 🧠 Human Factors & Usability Evaluation Are users struggling, making errors, or developing workarounds? I evaluate **human–system interaction** to identify where interfaces, procedures, and environments create friction—then I deliver actionable fixes. Typical work includes task analysis, workflow mapping, usability testing, cognitive walkthroughs, error analysis, and evidence-based recommendations for safer and more intuitive systems. ::: ::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6} ### 🥽 VR & Simulation for Human Performance I build and use **high-fidelity VR simulations** that let teams test designs and undergo training in realistic conditions—before products are deployed at scale. I support organisations that want to (a) establish VR capability, (b) prototype and evaluate procedures or interfaces, or (c) develop **bespoke simulations** for human factors research and performance training. ::: ::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6} ### 📊 Experimental Design, Measurement & Data Analysis If you need answers that hold up under scrutiny, I design studies that are **reproducible**, interpretable, and aligned to operational decisions. I build measurement plans, choose appropriate statistical models, and communicate results clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders. This is ideal for A/B tests, field trials, intervention evaluations, and any situation where you need credible evidence—not just opinions. ::: ::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6} ### 🤖 Human-AI Hybrid Workflow Design AI is transforming how teams work—but the hardest problem isn't the technology, it's designing workflows where **agentic AI systems and human judgement genuinely complement each other**. I help laboratories and companies architect hybrid workflows that leverage the speed, scalability, and consistency of AI agents while preserving the essential role of **human intuition, creativity, and ethical reasoning** at every level of distributed teamwork. The goal is augmentation with dignity—not replacement. ::: :::
